name: [NeuroQuest API] strict/IAP device-signature lockout spike — real installs blocked from export/erasure/purchases
query: logs("service:neuroquest-api env:production status:warn (\"device_signature_strict:missing\" OR \"device_signature_strict:invalid\" OR \"device_signature_strict:id_mismatch\" OR \"iap_device_auth:missing\" OR \"iap_device_auth:invalid\" OR \"iap_device_auth:id_mismatch\") @extra.active_install:true").index("*").rollup("count").last("15m") > 15

options.escalation_message: Active-install strict/IAP device-signature rejections are STILL above threshold. These paths IGNORE DEVICE_AUTH_SOFT_MODE — you must revert the offending deploy (gate or mobile signer); there is no flag-only mitigation.

_runbook.why: The existing device-signature-lockout monitor only watches the general per-:id gate (requireDeviceSignature). The GDPR export/erasure path (requireDeviceSignatureStrict) and the IAP entitlements path (requireUserOrDevice) reject device-signature failures INDEPENDENTLY and deliberately ignore DEVICE_AUTH_SOFT_MODE. A regression in either would lock real members out of data export or purchases with no alert. This monitor closes that coverage gap.
_runbook.signal: captureMessage('device_signature_strict:<status>', { extra: { active_install, scope:'gdpr_strict' } }) in artifacts/api-server/src/routes/app-user.ts and captureMessage('iap_device_auth:<status>', { extra: { active_install } }) in artifacts/api-server/src/routes/iap.ts. Only missing|invalid|id_mismatch indicate a wrongful lockout; expired (clock skew), issued_at_too_old (rotation), replayed (replay defense) and no_server_key (misconfig) are deliberately EXCLUDED.
_runbook.active_install_filter: @extra.active_install:true isolates device-aware clients (sent >=1 device-auth header) from the harmless steady tail of pre-handshake installs that send no headers and always land on `missing`. Without this filter the old-install tail would page. Same discriminator (isActiveInstall) the general lockout monitor uses.
_runbook.threshold_tuning: Production baseline of active-install rejections on these paths is ~0. warning=5 / critical=15 over a 15m window tolerates a handful of genuinely broken clients (corrupted keychain, extreme clock skew) while paging well before a systemic regression locks the member base out of export/erasure/purchases. Mirrors the general lockout monitor; revisit together if traffic grows materially.
_runbook.rollback: These paths IGNORE DEVICE_AUTH_SOFT_MODE by design (regulated data + purchase grants must not flow through the rollback path). The ONLY mitigation is to revert/redeploy the last-known-good build of the offending gate or mobile signer. See .agents/memory/device-signature-enforcement.md.
